claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex!

To continue assessing your eligibility, I have a few more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex's care and upbringing, and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex and they live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you and Alex currently living in the United Kingdom?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you.

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or approved training? And if relevant, could you tell me if Alex has any plans for education after age 16?**

(Since Alex is only 8, this is more for completeness – but it helps me confirm eligibility.)

Also, a couple more quick questions:

**What is your immigration status in the UK?** For example, are you a British or Irish citizen, or do you have settled status, or another type of visa?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Alex is in primary school, so yes, they're in full-time education - just normal school for an 8-year-old.

As for my immigration status, I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's very helpful.

Just one more question to ensure we've covered everything:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me and the eligibility rules, I can now make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

✅ **Alex is under 16** – at 8 years old, Alex clearly qualifies as a child for Child Benefit purposes

✅ **You are responsible for Alex** – Alex lives with you and you're responsible for their care

✅ **You both live in the UK** – you're residing in Manchester

✅ **You have the right to reside in the UK** – as a British citizen, you meet this requirement

✅ **No one else is claiming** – only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child, and no one else is currently claiming for Alex

You can apply for Child Benefit through the government's online service or by post. If you have any other questions about the application process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant is responsible for Alex and Alex lives with them. Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK (Manchester). The claimant is a British citizen, which means they have the right to reside in the UK. Alex is in full-time education (primary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit. They are a British citizen living in the UK, responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age of 16. The claimant is responsible for Alex and Alex lives with them. Both the claimant and Alex live in the UK (Manchester). The claimant is a British citizen, which means they have the right to reside in the UK. Alex is in full-time education (primary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit. They are a British citizen living in the UK, responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child.
